subject: DevOps
title: HashiCorp Vault
description: A quick, practical guide to HashiCorp Vault.

hook.text: Every microservice needs DB credentials. Managing them across 50 services by hand? Vault automates it.

buildup.text: Vault stores secrets encrypted, controls access with policies, and logs every access for auditing.

discovery.text: Dynamic secrets are Vault's superpower. It creates fresh credentials per request and auto-revokes them.

twist.text: If a dynamic credential leaks, blast radius is small. It serves one service and expires on its own.

climax.text: Vault plugs into Kubernetes, AWS IAM, and CI/CD tools. Secrets flow securely with no human touch.

punchline.text: Vault: encrypt, control, audit, rotate. Secrets done right.

quiz.question: What are dynamic secrets in HashiCorp Vault?
options: Credentials created on demand that expire automatically after a set time
Secrets that change their encryption key every hour
Passwords that are randomly displayed on a dashboard
